project_name: Memory Pressure Admission Gate Live Serving Validation

decision.recommended_next_action: Finalize this branch with the live llama.cpp validation artifacts and use them to decide whether to productize an OpenAI-compatible memory-gate proxy.
decision.stop_reason: A real local OpenAI-compatible llama.cpp endpoint completed paired baseline/gate workloads and the gate improved p95 latency by 69.02% and energy proxy by 70.95% with no completion loss.

recommended_next_action: Finalize this branch with the live llama.cpp validation artifacts and use them to decide whether to productize an OpenAI-compatible memory-gate proxy.
run_notes_tail: - It samples server RSS, GPU power via `nvidia-smi`, energy proxy, response latencies, completion rate, and response artifacts.
- Smoke-verified `/v1/models` and `/v1/chat/completions` on the llama.cpp endpoint.
- Final benchmark command: `python3 src/live_memory_gate_bench.py --base-url http://127.0.0.1:8011 --port 8011 --outdir results/live_llama_cpp_ctx8192`.
## Experiment results: llama.cpp Phi-4-mini GGUF, ctx 8192, 4 slots
| p95 request latency | 2.3336 s | 0.7230 s | 69.02% improvement |
| p95 arrival-to-finish latency | 2.9933 s | 0.9024 s | 69.85% improvement |
| Mean GPU power | 64.82 W | 34.32 W | 47.05% lower |
| Peak server RSS | 1257.30 MiB | 1337.33 MiB | 6.37% worse |

- Live serving evidence supports the gate on latency and energy: the memory gate exceeded the branch kill threshold by improving p95 latency and energy proxy by far more than 10% with no completion-rate loss.

- Peak server RSS did not improve in this llama.cpp run; it was 6.37% worse, likely because the process-level RSS includes model/runtime/prompt-cache allocation and is not a clean per-request KV pressure measure.
- The core validated mechanism in this run is admission-time compression of low-value long prompts before they occupy scarce slot context/KV budget, not a lower static model memory footprint.
- Evidence strength is moderate: it is a real local OpenAI-compatible endpoint with real request latencies and power sampling, but only one server stack/model and a compact deterministic workload.
